W-RRI/II/8/2008-@PS.1J 1, Gir ara aie ore — (1) ea eRe at taferar WH a aie, faa an wpe te fate ae (aaa aes am Sa gee | ag feu aqe fat a) aver 541(at), fers 17 epic te were & fee aga aieer) deine fafa, 2009 Here, 2008 Err sifergfas fara war aT | =e PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS REGULATORY (2) Geena ass F eH at aRRa S vaet Be BOARD i =: z NOTIFICATION eat >. he Se ath cea cin New Delhi, the 21st April, 2009 @ fae uga diem) faftas, 2008 & farran 12 & sa-fras G.S.R. 274(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred (2) # tiga & fer feafeiad at vfrenfte fear aren, by Section 61 of the Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory aa: Act, 2006 (19 of 2006), the Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board hereby makes the following regulations “ 2 ; 9 to amend the Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board aed fr so-fatreary (2) & siarit ate 33% stfahtat ama 4a SE ama fest fr Sam wa ae Ss (Access Code for Common Carrier or Contract Carrier Natu- . ral Gas Pipelines) Regulations, 2008, namely :-—~ eR fret was sacey Set 8 asa eH a Taft 1527 GI/2009 (1) -- 1 of 2 -- 2 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [Part I-—SEc. 3(i)] 1. Short title and commencement._{1) These regulations may be called the Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board (Access Code for Common Carrier or Contract Carrier Natural Gas Pipelines) Amendment Regulations, 2009. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Amendment in regulation. 12.— In regulation 12 of the Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board (Access Code ' for Common Carrier or Contract Carrier Natural Gas Pipelines) Regulations, 2008, for the proviso to sub- regulation (2), the following shall be substituted, namely :— “Provided that in case any capacity out of the 33% excess capacity under sub-regulation (2) is available at any time due to non-existence of demand from any shipper, then, the same may be contracted for a period of one year or more subject to the stipulation that in case another entity seeks booking of the same for a period of less than one year, the request shall be accommodated after pro-rating the same from the common carrier capacity already contracted to other entities for a period of one year or more: Provided further that pro-rating the common carrier capacity shall not exceed ten per cent of the total common carrier capacity.” [F. No. S-Admn/I/8/2008-Vol. 1] RATAN P. WATAL, Secy. Foot Note :— Principal regulations were notified vide G.S.R. 541(E), dated 17th July, 2008.
